data.title: Arbitrum project team: Has increased its stake in ARB according to the established plan.
data.description: Offchain Labs stated in a post on the X platform that it is committed to developing the Arbitrum ecosystem in a practical and effective way and believes that continued direct investment is in its best interest. Therefore, Offchain Labs has increased its holdings of ARB according to its established purchase plan. Through these actions, Offchain Labs will double down on its investment in the development of Arbitrum across all aspects. Offchain Labs firmly believes in the DAO's strategic initiatives: empowering developers, building the community, and driving technological progress.

data.title: Bitcoin Dominance Rises Amid Narrowing Market Structure
data.description:
According to Odaily, Wintermute has noted on the X platform that as the holiday season approaches, the market structure is narrowing, leading to an increase in Bitcoin's dominance. The oversupply and intensive token unlocking schedules have resulted in the continued underperformance of altcoins. Internal liquidity data indicates that there is buying pressure on major cryptocurrencies, with institutional funds consistently purchasing since the summer, while retail investors are shifting from altcoins back to mainstream cryptocurrencies.

Despite spot buyers providing a stable foundation for the market, price discovery is still occurring through derivatives. This has resulted in a coexistence of net buying of major cryptocurrencies and intraday sharp declines triggered by leveraged position liquidations. Additionally, traditional financial institutions are steadily entering the crypto space, which is expected to support mid-term prices. In the short term, the market is expected to remain volatile due to thin liquidity, with Bitcoin (BTC) and Ethereum (ETH) continuing to act as primary risk absorbers.

data.title: Japanese 30-Year Government Bond Yield Reaches Record High
data.description: According to Odaily, the yield on Japan's 30-year government bonds has increased by 2 basis points, reaching a historic high of 3.445%. This development marks a significant milestone in the country's financial markets.

data.title: Investor Increases Ethereum Holdings Amid Price Fluctuations
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, recent monitoring by HyperInsight reveals that over the past 10 hours, investor Huang Licheng has increased his Ethereum (ETH) holdings by 475 units, with the price ranging between $2,932 and $2,942. This expansion has raised his position by approximately $1.4 million. Subsequently, he placed sell orders within the $2,960 to $3,125 range, some of which have already been executed.

The monitoring indicates that since December 17, Huang has consistently held long positions in ETH, frequently engaging in strategic buying and selling to increase his holdings. His current ETH position has grown from $13.2 million to $21.62 million, with an average price of $2,978, resulting in an unrealized loss of about $110,000, or roughly 13%. The liquidation price is set at $2,870. Additionally, Huang has fully closed his previous long positions in HYPE and ZEC.

data.title: Trend Research Acquires 46,379 ETH Using Leverage
data.description: According to Odaily, Trend Research, a secondary investment institution under Jackyi_ld, has recently purchased 46,379 ETH using leverage, valued at $137 million. The firm began accumulating ETH in early November when the price was around $3,400. Currently, Trend Research holds a total of 580,000 ETH, worth $1.72 billion, with an average cost of approximately $3,208 per ETH, resulting in an unrealized loss of $141 million. The acquisition was made using borrowed funds, with the institution securing $887 million USDT from Aave, employing a leverage ratio of about 2x. Previously, Trend Research profited $24.48 million from shorting ETH.

data.title: Gold-Silver Ratio Reaches Lowest Level Since 2014
data.description: According to BlockBeats, market data indicates that the gold-silver ratio has fallen to 62.35, marking its lowest point since July 2014. Earlier this year, in late April, the ratio was at a high of 106.

data.title: Bitget Wallet integrates with Hyperliquid, extending on-chain perpetual contract trading capabilities.
data.description: Bitget Wallet has completed its integration with the decentralized perpetual contract trading platform Hyperliquid, further enhancing its on-chain derivatives trading capabilities. Following the integration, Bitget Wallet's perpetual contract functionality will offer lower fees, a wider range of asset classes, and support for a customizable, centralized exchange-like trading interface, while maintaining its self-custody characteristics. Reportedly, after this integration, perpetual contract trading fees within Bitget Wallet can be reduced to 0.06%–0.09%, supporting trading pairs for over 300 crypto assets and introducing RWA perpetual contracts, including tokenized stocks. It supports leverage up to 150x and displays margin requirements, open positions, and risk exposure simultaneously when placing an order. In terms of user experience, the new version supports configurable candlestick charts and order book layouts, simplifying the trading process and lowering the barrier to entry for users participating in on-chain perpetual contract trading. It provides users with a more professional trading platform-like experience in a self-custody environment. According to on-chain data, Bitget Wallet's perpetual contract trading volume exceeded $8 billion in the fourth quarter of 2025. This transaction is an important step for Bitget Wallet to deepen its on-chain derivatives service offerings and cover more trading needs for crypto assets and tokenized assets.

data.title: Trend Research borrowed to buy 46,000 ETH, and currently holds a total of 580,000 ETH with a paper loss of $141 million.
data.description: According to Ember Monitoring, Trend Research, a secondary investment firm under Jackyi_ld, purchased 46,379 ETH today using leverage, worth $137 million. The firm began buying ETH in early November when it was around $3,400, and currently holds a total of 580,000 ETH, worth $1.72 billion, with an average cost of approximately $3,208. It is currently experiencing a paper loss of $141 million. Trend Research used leverage to purchase these assets, borrowing a total of 887 million USDT from Aave, a leverage ratio of approximately 2. The firm previously profited $24.48 million by shorting ETH.

data.title: U.S. Regulatory Developments Signal Shift in Crypto Market Framework
data.description:
According to PANews, recent developments in the U.S. crypto market indicate significant changes in regulatory and legislative approaches. Over the past 24 hours, two major advancements have emerged that could reshape the industry landscape.

Firstly, regulatory certainty has been enhanced with Michael Selig officially assuming the role of CFTC Chairman. He announced that Congress is prepared to present a crypto market structure bill to the President's desk. This move marks a pivotal shift in the regulatory power dynamics between the SEC and CFTC, aiming to end the longstanding jurisdictional disputes and enforcement-driven chaos.

Secondly, a new bipartisan legislative proposal seeks to reduce transaction costs by exempting certain stablecoin payments from capital gains tax and allowing a five-year deferral on staking income recognition. Although still in draft form, these proposals highlight lawmakers' intent to address the practical challenges of crypto payments and encourage long-term holding by easing cash flow pressures.

These developments are not isolated. They align with the recent approval of a DTCC pilot project, which integrates tokenized assets into the clearing system, forming a comprehensive compliance loop from infrastructure to application scenarios.

The CFTC's legislative push complements the SEC's recent initiative to classify digital commodities like Bitcoin and Ethereum outside the securities domain. This coordinated effort aims to transition regulatory logic from SEC's enforcement-driven approach to CFTC's legislative-driven framework.

The proposed tax legislation addresses key application challenges by potentially eliminating tax barriers in crypto payments, allowing them to function more as currency rather than assets in commercial activities. The deferral of staking income recognition is designed to alleviate cash flow burdens and promote long-term asset holding.

These actions collectively illustrate a strategic shift in the U.S. crypto landscape, encompassing technological, legal, and tax dimensions to systematically remove obstacles. As regulatory frameworks become clearer and potential tax frictions are addressed, the large-scale adoption of crypto assets is becoming a more tangible reality.

The DTCC's blockchain integration offers reassurance to financial institutions, while the tax proposal's progress provides a promising outlook for commercial applications. The future crypto world may integrate more seamlessly into the global financial system, characterized by greater compliance and reduced friction.

*This content is for informational purposes only and does not constitute investment advice. Markets carry risks, and investments should be made cautiously.*
